title: Connect and Operate MCP in Codex and Claude · Protocol Clinic: Support Runbook
summary: Configure one safe server in both Codex and Claude Code. Planned artifact: Add and verify the agent ext support runbook increment in cross-client MCP configuration and smoke tests. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Create a connection, auth, timeout, output-limit, and version troubleshooting runbook.
Document client-specific config, approval, prompt, resource, and output behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Build Support Runbook evidence
steps.instruction: Append a agent-ext-support-runbook-qqipn1: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Support Runbook into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
agent-ext-support-runbook-qqipn1:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Defend the Support Runbook model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Support Runbook applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Create a connection, auth, timeout, output-limit, and version troubleshooting runbook.
